structured.research_question:
Does 4-week supplementation of C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus
GG measurably reduce intestinal permeability via upregulation of tight
junction proteins?
structured.subject: C57BL/6 mice (intestinal epithelium)
structured.independent:
Daily oral supplementation with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG (vs. vehicle
control) for 4 weeks.
structured.dependent:
Intestinal permeability, measured by FITC-dextran flux assay; tight
junction protein expression (claudin-1 and occludin) measured by Western
blot.
structured.conditions:
n>=8 per group, age-matched 8-10 week old C57BL/6 mice. FITC-dextran
(4kDa) gavage 4h before terminal blood draw. Standard chow diet, 12h
light cycle.
structured.expected:
L. rhamnosus GG supplementation reduces intestinal permeability by at
least 30% compared to controls, with corresponding upregulation of
claudin-1 and occludin.

Standard vivarium facilities and experienced animal handlers are available.
All necessary reagents and equipment for molecular biology (Western blot) and fluorescence assays are available.
Researchers are proficient in oral gavage and tissue dissection techniques.

procedures.name: Animal Acclimation and Grouping
procedures.intent: To acclimate C57BL/6 mice to the vivarium environment and randomly assign them to treatment and control groups.

steps.title: Receive and Acclimate Mice
steps.body_md: Upon arrival, C57BL/6 mice (age 8-10 weeks) should be housed in a controlled vivarium environment for at least 7 days prior to any experimental intervention. Mice should be kept in groups of up to five per cage with appropriate bedding material.
steps.notes: Ensure all animal care and handling procedures comply with institutional animal care guidelines.

steps.title: Control Environmental Conditions
steps.body_md: Maintain the vivarium environment with a controlled temperature between 21-24°C and humidity between 50-60%. Implement a 12-hour light/12-hour dark cycle throughout the acclimation and experimental periods.
equipment_needed: HVAC system with temperature and humidity control

steps.title: Provide Food and Water Ad Libitum
steps.body_md: During acclimation and the experimental period, provide standard chow diet and reverse osmosis (RO) water ad libitum to all mice.

steps.title: Randomly Assign Mice to Experimental Groups
steps.body_md: After the acclimation period, randomly assign the C57BL/6 mice into two experimental groups: a `Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG` supplementation group and a `Vehicle Control` group. Each group must contain at least 8 mice.
equipment_needed: Computer with random number generator or similar random assignment method
todo_for_researcher: Confirmation of exact n per group and final randomization numbers.
steps.notes: Ensure that the randomization process is documented and blinded if possible for subsequent measurements.

steps.title: Individualize Mouse Identification
steps.body_md: Identify each mouse within its assigned group using appropriate and humane identification methods (e.g., ear tags, microchipping, or permanent marker on the tail). Record corresponding cage and group assignments for each mouse.
todo_for_researcher: Selection of preferred humane identification method.

equipment: HVAC system with temperature and humidity control
Computer with random number generator or similar random assignment method

success_criteria.threshold: No significant weight loss (>10% body weight) and absence of clinical signs of distress/illness.

success_criteria.how_measured: Documentation of randomization process and final group assignments.

procedures.name: Daily Oral Supplementation
procedures.intent: To administer Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG (LGG) or vehicle control daily via oral gavage for 4 weeks.

steps.title: Prepare Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG (LGG) suspension
steps.body_md: Prepare a fresh suspension of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG (LGG) in physiological saline daily. The final concentration should be such that a 200 µL oral gavage dose delivers the target CFU/day (e.g., 10^9 CFU/day). Ensure all procedures maintain bacterial viability.
todo_for_researcher: Determine the specific CFU/day dose for LGG
Establish optimal resuspension protocol for LGG to maintain viability and achieve target concentration from frozen stock or culture
steps.notes: The exact CFU/day for LGG needs to be specified based on prior literature or pilot studies for optimal efficacy in reducing intestinal permeability.

steps.title: Prepare Vehicle Control (Physiological Saline)
steps.body_md: For the vehicle control group, prepare an equivalent volume (200 µL) of sterile physiological saline (0.9% NaCl) daily. Ensure sterility.
steps.notes: Physiological saline is used as the vehicle to match the LGG suspension preparation.

steps.title: Acclimate Mice (Pre-Experiment)
steps.body_md: Ensure C57BL/6 mice (8-10 weeks old, male or female depending on experimental design, n>=8 per group) are acclimatized to the animal facility for at least one week prior to the start of daily oral supplementation. Maintain standard housing conditions: sawdust bedding, ad libitum access to water and standard chow diet, controlled temperature (21-24°C), humidity (50-60%), and a 12-hour light/12-hour dark cycle.
todo_for_researcher: Specify the exact number of mice per group (n>=8 minimum)
steps.notes: Acclimation minimizes stress, which can impact physiological parameters like intestinal permeability. Age and strain are critical for experimental consistency.

steps.title: Randomize and Assign Mice to Groups
steps.body_md: Randomly assign the acclimatized C57BL/6 mice into two groups: one receiving LGG supplementation and one receiving the vehicle control. Ensure equal distribution of age and weight (if applicable) across groups. Each group should have a minimum of 8 mice.
steps.notes: Randomization is critical to prevent bias.

steps.title: Daily Oral Gavage of LGG Group
steps.body_md: Beginning on Day 1, orally administer 200 µL of the freshly prepared LGG suspension (at the determined CFU/day dose) to mice in the LGG group once daily for 4 consecutive weeks. Gently restrain each mouse and carefully introduce a sterile gavage needle into the esophagus, ensuring proper placement before dispensing the solution slowly to avoid aspiration.
todo_for_researcher: Confirm optimal gavage needle size for C57BL/6 mice
steps.notes: Consistent daily administration is crucial for the intervention's efficacy. Proper gavage technique is essential to minimize stress and prevent injury or aspiration.

steps.title: Daily Oral Gavage of Control Group
steps.body_md: Concurrently with the LGG group, orally administer 200 µL of sterile physiological saline to mice in the vehicle control group once daily for 4 consecutive weeks, using the same gavage technique.
todo_for_researcher: Confirm optimal gavage needle size for C57BL/6 mice
steps.notes: This group controls for the effects of the gavage procedure itself and the vehicle.

steps.title: Monitor Animal Health and Weight
steps.body_md: Throughout the 4-week supplementation period, monitor all mice daily for general health, body weight changes, and any signs of adverse effects (e.g., severe lethargy, changes in fur condition, diarrhea, or reduced food/water intake). Record observations.
steps.notes: Ensures animal welfare and allows for early detection of potential issues related to the intervention or gavage. Significant weight loss or illness may necessitate removal from the study.

success_criteria.how_measured: Documentation of all gavages administered for each mouse in both groups over the 4-week period.
success_criteria.threshold: 100% of planned daily gavages completed for all mice still in the study.

success_criteria.how_measured: Daily health checks and weekly body weight measurements.
success_criteria.threshold: No significant adverse effects (e.g., >10% body weight loss, signs of severe distress or illness) requiring removal from study; if adverse events occur, they should be documented and approved by veterinary staff.

procedures.name: Intestinal Permeability Assay
procedures.intent: To measure intestinal permeability using a FITC-dextran flux assay at the end of the supplementation period.

steps.title: Prepare FITC-Dextran Solution
steps.body_md: Prepare a 60 mg/mL solution of FITC-dextran (4 kDa) in sterile PBS. Vortex thoroughly to ensure complete dissolution. Store in the dark at 4°C until use. This concentration is chosen to ensure sufficient detection in serum at the specified gavage volume.

steps.title: Fast Mice Prior to Gavage
steps.body_md: Fast all mice (both control and supplemented groups) for 4 hours prior to oral gavage with FITC-dextran. Ensure ad libitum access to water during the fasting period. Fasting ensures consistent gastric emptying and absorption kinetics.

steps.title: Administer FITC-Dextran by Oral Gavage
steps.body_md: Carefully restrain each mouse and administer 0.6 mL of the 60 mg/mL FITC-dextran solution (prepared in step 1) via oral gavage using a sterile gavage needle. Ensure accurate delivery and observe for any signs of aspiration. The gavage volume and concentration are selected to provide an adequate dose for detection in the serum.
todo_for_researcher: Specify appropriate gavage needle size for C57BL/6 mice.

steps.title: Post-Gavage Incubation
steps.body_md: After gavage, return mice to their cages with continued access to water (and food if appropriate per experimental design, though fasting for the full 4 hours before blood collection is recommended) for a 4-hour incubation period. This allows sufficient time for intestinal absorption and circulation of FITC-dextran.

steps.title: Perform Terminal Blood Collection
steps.body_md: At exactly 4 hours post-gavage, perform terminal blood collection from each mouse. Euthanize mice using an IACUC-approved method (e.g., CO2 asphyxiation followed by cervical dislocation). Collect blood via cardiac puncture or retro-orbital sinus into a sterile microcentrifuge tube, ensuring collection into plain tubes (no anticoagulant) if serum is desired, or tubes with anticoagulant (e.g., EDTA or heparin) if plasma is desired. Aim for maximum blood collection (typically 0.5-1 mL per mouse).
equipment_needed: Sterile microcentrifuge tubes (with or without anticoagulant)
Sterile needles and syringes (for blood collection)
todo_for_researcher: Confirm IACUC-approved euthanasia and blood collection protocols for C57BL/6 mice.

steps.title: Process Blood to Obtain Serum
steps.body_md: Allow collected blood to clot at room temperature for 30 minutes (if using plain tubes). Centrifuge blood samples at 2000 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C. Carefully aspirate the supernatant (serum) into new, labeled microcentrifuge tubes, avoiding disturbance of the red blood cell pellet. If working with plasma (collected using anticoagulant), centrifuge immediately after collection without clotting.

steps.title: Store Serum Samples
steps.body_md: Store isolated serum samples at -80°C until further analysis. Avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles, which can degrade the fluorophore or affect protein-FITC binding.

steps.title: Measure FITC-Dextran Fluorescence
steps.body_md: Thaw serum samples on ice. Pipette 100 µL of each serum sample into a black, opaque 96-well plate. Measure the fluorescence intensity using a fluorescence microplate reader with an excitation wavelength of 485 nm and an emission wavelength of 535 nm. Include a standard curve of known FITC-dextran concentrations in mouse serum (or PBS if serum matrix effects are negligible or accounted for) on each plate.
controls: FITC-dextran standard curve (in mouse serum or PBS)
todo_for_researcher: Determine appropriate range for FITC-dextran standard curve in mouse serum.
Confirm optimal gain settings for fluorescence plate reader.

steps.title: Calculate Intestinal Permeability
steps.body_md: Generate a standard curve from the known FITC-dextran concentrations and their measured fluorescence intensities. Use this standard curve to determine the concentration of FITC-dextran in each experimental serum sample. The serum concentration of FITC-dextran is directly proportional to intestinal permeability. Express results as µg/mL of FITC-dextran in serum or as fold change over vehicle control.
todo_for_researcher: Specify appropriate data analysis software (e.g., GraphPad Prism, Excel).

equipment: Sterile microcentrifuge tubes (with or without anticoagulant)
Sterile needles and syringes (for blood collection)

success_criteria.how_measured: No visible signs of aspiration, clear administration into the esophagus
Sufficient volume of serum (e.g., >100 µL) collected per mouse, free of significant hemolysis.
success_criteria.threshold: At least 100 µL serum per mouse, hemolysis minimal (as judged visually)

success_criteria.how_measured: Fluorescence values in serum samples are above background noise and within the linear range of the standard curve.
success_criteria.threshold: Fluorescence values > 2x background, and within min/max of standard curve

success_criteria.how_measured: R-squared value of the FITC-dextran standard curve.

procedures.name: Tissue and Blood Collection
procedures.intent: To collect blood, ileum, and colon tissue samples for subsequent analysis of FITC-dextran, and tight junction proteins.

steps.title: Euthanize mice
steps.body_md: Euthanize mice according to institutional guidelines. The preferred method is CO2 inhalation in a gradually filled chamber. Ensure complete cessation of respiration and heartbeat before proceeding.
todo_for_researcher: Verify CO2 flow rate for gradual unconsciousness and euthanasia.

steps.title: Perform terminal blood collection
steps.body_md: Immediately after euthanasia, perform terminal blood collection. The preferred method is cardiac puncture. Collect blood using a syringe with a 23 gauge needle into a serum separator tube. Gently invert the tubes 5-6 times. Alternatively, perform retro-orbital blood collection if cardiac puncture is not feasible. This blood will be used for FITC-dextran analysis.
steps.notes: Ensure quick collection to prevent coagulation.

steps.title: Prepare blood for serum separation
steps.body_md: Allow the collected blood to clot at room temperature for 30 minutes. After clotting, centrifuge the serum separator tubes at 2000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C to separate serum.

steps.title: Store serum samples
steps.body_md: Carefully aliquot the clear serum supernatant into cryovials and immediately flash-freeze in liquid nitrogen. Store samples at -80°C until further analysis for FITC-dextran.
steps.notes: Avoid freeze-thaw cycles.

steps.title: Disinfect and open abdominal cavity
steps.body_md: Place the euthanized mouse in a supine position. Disinfect the lower abdomen thoroughly with a 70% ethanol solution. Make a vertical midline incision of approximately 5 cm through the skin and abdominal muscle layers to expose the abdominal cavity.
steps.notes: Ensure sterile technique where possible to prevent contamination of tissues if secondary analyses are planned.

steps.title: Locate and excise small intestine
steps.body_md: Gently move aside the organs to visualize the small intestine. Carefully disentangle the ileum from its mesenteric attachments and excise approximately 2 cm of the distal ileum using fine scissors. Place the ileum segment in a sterile dish on ice.
steps.notes: Minimize handling of the tissue to preserve its integrity.

steps.title: Flush and clean ileum segment
steps.body_md: Place the excised ileum segment on a sterile surface. Gently flush the lumen of the ileum with ice-cold phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) using a blunt-ended needle and syringe to remove intestinal contents. Ensure thorough cleaning without damaging the tissue.

steps.title: Collect and store ileum tissue
steps.body_md: Cut the cleaned ileum segment into two pieces. Flash-freeze one piece in liquid nitrogen for Western blot analysis (claudin-1, occludin). Place the other piece into a cryovial containing a suitable fixative (e.g., 10% neutral buffered formalin) if histology is required, otherwise flash-freeze for other analyses. Store flash-frozen samples at -80°C. If fixed, store at 4°C until further processing.
equipment_needed: Container with 10% neutral buffered formalin (if needed)
todo_for_researcher: Confirm whether histology is required for ileum and colon, and thus whether to fix tissue or only flash-freeze.

steps.title: Locate and excise colon
steps.body_md: After ileum collection, locate the colon. Carefully excise approximately 2 cm of the distal colon (excluding the cecum and rectum) using fine scissors. Place the colon segment in a sterile dish on ice.
steps.notes: Avoid contamination from fecal matter.

steps.title: Flush and clean colon segment
steps.body_md: Place the excised colon segment on a sterile surface. Gently flush the lumen of the colon with ice-cold phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) using a blunt-ended needle and syringe to remove intestinal contents. Ensure thorough cleaning without damaging the tissue.

steps.title: Collect and store colon tissue
steps.body_md: Cut the cleaned colon segment into two pieces. Flash-freeze one piece in liquid nitrogen for Western blot analysis (claudin-1, occludin). Place the other piece into a cryovial containing a suitable fixative (e.g., 10% neutral buffered formalin) if histology is required, otherwise flash-freeze for other analyses. Store flash-frozen samples at -80°C. If fixed, store at 4°C until further processing.
equipment_needed: Container with 10% neutral buffered formalin (if needed)
todo_for_researcher: Confirm whether histology is required for ileum and colon, and thus whether to fix tissue or only flash-freeze.

procedures.name: FITC-Dextran Measurement
procedures.intent: To quantify FITC-dextran concentration in serum samples as a measure of intestinal permeability.

steps.title: Prepare FITC-Dextran Standards
steps.body_md: Prepare a series of FITC-dextran (4 kDa) standards ranging from 0.05 µg/mL to 50 µg/mL using pooled mouse serum (from untreated mice or a commercial source) as the diluent. Make at least 7-8 serial dilutions. Ensure accuracy in pipetting. Prepare each standard in duplicate.
todo_for_researcher: Determine the exact number and concentration of standards to create a robust standard curve based on expected sample concentrations.
steps.notes: Using pooled mouse serum as the diluent helps account for potential fluorescence interference from serum components, providing a more accurate standard curve for serum samples.

steps.title: Plate Standards and Samples
steps.body_md: Pipette 100 µL of each prepared FITC-dextran standard into designated wells of a black, opaque 96-well plate. Ensure standards are plated in duplicate or triplicate. Subsequently, pipette 100 µL of each unknown serum sample (collected from C57BL/6 mice) into separate wells. Ensure samples are also plated in duplicate. Include wells with 100 µL of pooled mouse serum only (without FITC-dextran) as a blank.
steps.notes: Black, opaque plates minimize well-to-well crosstalk and background fluorescence, which is crucial for sensitive fluorescence measurements.

steps.title: Measure Fluorescence
steps.body_md: Place the 96-well plate into a fluorescence plate reader. Set the excitation wavelength to 485 nm and the emission wavelength to 528 nm. Measure the fluorescence intensity for all wells. Ensure the gain setting of the plate reader is optimized to maximize signal differentiation without saturating the detector. The plate should remain at room temperature during measurement.
todo_for_researcher: Specify the exact model of fluorescence plate reader and any specific software settings required.
steps.notes: The specified excitation/emission wavelengths are standard for FITC-dextran. Optimizing gain is critical for accurate quantification across the wide range of concentrations.

steps.title: Generate Standard Curve
steps.body_md: Subtract the average fluorescence of the blank wells (pooled mouse serum only) from all standard and sample readings. Plot the average corrected fluorescence intensity of the FITC-dextran standards against their known concentrations to generate a standard curve. Typically, a linear or sigmoidal fit (e.g., a 4-parameter logistic curve) is appropriate, ensuring a high R-squared value (>0.99).
equipment_needed: Computer with graphing software (e.g., GraphPad Prism, Excel)
todo_for_researcher: Specify the preferred graphing software and data analysis method (e.g., linear regression, 4PL).
steps.notes: A robust standard curve is essential for accurately interpolating the concentrations of unknown samples. Correcting for background fluorescence from serum blanks improves accuracy.

steps.title: Calculate Sample Concentrations
steps.body_md: Use the generated standard curve equation to interpolate the FITC-dextran concentration (µg/mL) for each unknown serum sample based on its corrected fluorescence intensity reading. Report concentrations for all replicates and then average them for each sample.
equipment_needed: Computer with graphing software (e.g., GraphPad Prism, Excel)
steps.notes: Record raw fluorescence values and calculated concentrations for all samples for future troubleshooting or re-analysis.

equipment: Computer with graphing software (e.g., GraphPad Prism, Excel)
reagents: FITC-dextran (4 kDa, molecular weight as specified in hypothesis)
Pooled mouse serum (from untreated C57BL/6 mice or commercial source)
Mouse serum samples (from experimental C57BL/6 mice)

success_criteria.how_measured: R-squared value of the fitted standard curve
Fluorescence intensity of blank wells (pooled mouse serum without FITC-dextran)
success_criteria.threshold: Low, typically < 500 relative fluorescence units (RFU) or within 10% of the lowest standard concentration's RFU

success_criteria.how_measured: Measurable FITC-dextran concentrations in samples expected to have increased permeability (e.g., positive controls if applicable, or compared to vehicle control group)
success_criteria.threshold: Positive control samples (if available) should show significantly higher concentrations than negative controls.

procedures.name: Western Blot for Tight Junction Proteins
procedures.intent: To extract proteins from intestinal tissue and quantify the expression of Claudin-1 and Occludin.

steps.title: Tissue Homogenization
steps.body_md: Upon receipt of intestinal tissue (ileum and/or colon segments), immediately place tissue on ice. Add 500 μL of RIPA lysis buffer (containing protease and phosphatase inhibitors) to the tissue. Homogenize the tissue using a tissue homogenizer (e.g., Bead Ruptor 24 Elite) at 4.0 m/s for 30 seconds, repeating for 3 cycles with 1-minute intervals on ice between cycles to prevent overheating.
todo_for_researcher: Identify specific cat# and vendor for protease and phosphatase inhibitor cocktails suitable for intestinal tissue proteomics.
steps.notes: Ensure all buffers are pre-chilled and kept on ice during the entire process to prevent protein degradation.

steps.title: Cell Lysis and Centrifugation
steps.body_md: Following homogenization, incubate the samples on ice for 30 minutes to facilitate complete cell lysis. Afterward, centrifuge the homogenates at 12,000 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C to pellet cellular debris.
steps.notes: The supernatant contains the total protein extract.

steps.title: Protein Quantification
steps.body_md: Carefully transfer the supernatant (containing the protein lysate) to a new pre-chilled microcentrifuge tube. Determine the protein concentration using the BCA protein assay according to the manufacturer's instructions. Prepare a standard curve using bovine serum albumin (BSA).
steps.notes: Accurate protein quantification is critical for equal loading in subsequent SDS-PAGE.

steps.title: Sample Preparation for SDS-PAGE
steps.body_md: Normalize all protein samples to a concentration of 2-3 μg/μL using RIPA lysis buffer. Add an appropriate volume of 4X Laemmli sample buffer (containing β-mercaptoethanol) to achieve a final 1X concentration. Heat the samples at 95°C for 5 minutes.
steps.notes: Proper denaturation is essential for good protein separation.

steps.title: SDS-PAGE Electrophoresis
steps.body_md: Load 20 μg of protein per well onto a 10% SDS-PAGE gel. Include a pre-stained protein ladder to estimate protein molecular weights. Run the gel at 100-120V until the dye front reaches the bottom of the gel (approximately 1.5-2 hours).
equipment_needed: Gel casting apparatus (if making own gels)
todo_for_researcher: Specify commercial vendor and catalog number for pre-cast 10% SDS-PAGE gels or detailed recipe for in-house preparation.
steps.notes: Ensure even loading to prevent artifacts in quantification.

steps.title: Protein Transfer to PVDF Membrane
steps.body_md: After electrophoresis, equilibrate the gel and a PVDF membrane in transfer buffer for 10 minutes. Assemble the transfer sandwich according to the manufacturer's instructions. Transfer proteins from the gel to the PVDF membrane using a wet transfer system at 100V for 1 hour at 4°C.
steps.notes: PVDF membranes require pre-wetting in methanol. Maintain a cold environment during transfer to prevent overheating.

steps.title: Blocking
steps.body_md: Remove the PVDF membrane from the transfer apparatus and immediately immerse it in 5% non-fat dry milk in TBST (Tris-buffered saline with 0.1% Tween 20). Block the membrane for 1 hour at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.notes: Blocking prevents non-specific antibody binding.

steps.title: Primary Antibody Incubation
steps.body_md: Discard the blocking solution. Incubate the membrane overnight at 4°C with primary antibodies diluted in 5% BSA in TBST. Use the following dilutions: Anti-Claudin-1 (1:1000), Anti-Occludin (1:1000), and Anti-GAPDH or Anti-β-Actin (1:5000) as a loading control.
todo_for_researcher: Identify specific clone and vendor for Claudin-1, Occludin, and loading control antibodies (e.g., Cell Signaling Technology, Abcam).
Confirm whether GAPDH or beta-actin is a more suitable loading control for intestinal tissue under experimental conditions.
steps.notes: Overnight incubation at 4°C typically yields better signal-to-noise ratio.

steps.title: Washing
steps.body_md: After primary antibody incubation, wash the membrane three times for 10 minutes each with TBST at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.notes: Thorough washing is crucial to remove unbound primary antibodies and reduce background signal.

steps.title: Secondary Antibody Incubation
steps.body_md: Incubate the membrane with appropriate HRP-conjugated secondary antibodies (e.g., anti-rabbit HRP for Claudin-1 and Occludin if primary antibodies are raised in rabbit, and anti-mouse HRP for the loading control if raised in mouse) diluted 1:10,000 in 5% non-fat dry milk in TBST for 1 hour at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.notes: Ensure secondary antibodies match the host species of the primary antibodies.

steps.title: Final Washing
steps.body_md: Wash the membrane three times for 10 minutes each with TBST, followed by two additional washes for 5 minutes each with TBS (without Tween 20) to remove residual detergent. All washes should be at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.notes: The final TBS washes help to reduce background and improve signal in detection.

steps.title: Chemiluminescent Detection
steps.body_md: Incubate the membrane with enhanced chemiluminescence (ECL) substrate for 1-5 minutes according to the manufacturer's instructions. Detect immunobands using a chemiluminescence imaging system. Capture images and ensure non-saturated signals for all bands, especially the loading control.
steps.notes: Optimize exposure time to get appropriate signal intensity without saturation for quantitative analysis.

steps.title: Densitometric Analysis
steps.body_md: Quantify the band intensities using appropriate densitometric software (e.g., ImageJ or Bio-Rad Image Lab software). Normalize the intensity of Claudin-1 and Occludin bands to the corresponding loading control (GAPDH or β-Actin) for each sample. Report relative protein expression levels.
steps.notes: Perform background subtraction and ensure linear range of detection for accurate quantification.

reagents: HRP-conjugated secondary antibodies (e.g., anti-rabbit HRP, anti-mouse HRP)

success_criteria.how_measured: BCA assay
Clear and distinct protein bands with ladder visible
Visualization of protein ladder on membrane and absence of significant protein on gel after transfer (Coomassie stain optional)
Presence of distinct bands for Claudin-1, Occludin, and loading control at expected molecular weights, with non-saturated signals suitable for quantification.
Relative protein expression values normalized to loading control
success_criteria.threshold: Coefficient of variation for technical replicates < 15%

steps.title: Receive and Acclimate Mice
steps.body_md: Upon arrival, C57BL/6 mice (age 8-10 weeks) should be housed in a controlled vivarium environment for at least 7 days prior to any experimental intervention. Mice should be kept in groups of up to five per cage with appropriate bedding material.
steps.notes: Ensure all animal care and handling procedures comply with institutional animal care guidelines.

steps.title: Control Environmental Conditions
steps.body_md: Maintain the vivarium environment with a controlled temperature between 21-24°C and humidity between 50-60%. Implement a 12-hour light/12-hour dark cycle throughout the acclimation and experimental periods.
equipment_needed: HVAC system with temperature and humidity control

steps.title: Provide Food and Water Ad Libitum
steps.body_md: During acclimation and the experimental period, provide standard chow diet and reverse osmosis (RO) water ad libitum to all mice.

steps.title: Randomly Assign Mice to Experimental Groups
steps.body_md: After the acclimation period, randomly assign the C57BL/6 mice into two experimental groups: a `Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG` supplementation group and a `Vehicle Control` group. Each group must contain at least 8 mice.
equipment_needed: Computer with random number generator or similar random assignment method
todo_for_researcher: Confirmation of exact n per group and final randomization numbers.
steps.notes: Ensure that the randomization process is documented and blinded if possible for subsequent measurements.

steps.title: Individualize Mouse Identification
steps.body_md: Identify each mouse within its assigned group using appropriate and humane identification methods (e.g., ear tags, microchipping, or permanent marker on the tail). Record corresponding cage and group assignments for each mouse.
todo_for_researcher: Selection of preferred humane identification method.

steps.title: Prepare Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG (LGG) suspension
steps.body_md: Prepare a fresh suspension of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG (LGG) in physiological saline daily. The final concentration should be such that a 200 µL oral gavage dose delivers the target CFU/day (e.g., 10^9 CFU/day). Ensure all procedures maintain bacterial viability.
todo_for_researcher: Determine the specific CFU/day dose for LGG
Establish optimal resuspension protocol for LGG to maintain viability and achieve target concentration from frozen stock or culture
steps.notes: The exact CFU/day for LGG needs to be specified based on prior literature or pilot studies for optimal efficacy in reducing intestinal permeability.

steps.title: Prepare Vehicle Control (Physiological Saline)
steps.body_md: For the vehicle control group, prepare an equivalent volume (200 µL) of sterile physiological saline (0.9% NaCl) daily. Ensure sterility.
steps.notes: Physiological saline is used as the vehicle to match the LGG suspension preparation.

steps.title: Acclimate Mice (Pre-Experiment)
steps.body_md: Ensure C57BL/6 mice (8-10 weeks old, male or female depending on experimental design, n>=8 per group) are acclimatized to the animal facility for at least one week prior to the start of daily oral supplementation. Maintain standard housing conditions: sawdust bedding, ad libitum access to water and standard chow diet, controlled temperature (21-24°C), humidity (50-60%), and a 12-hour light/12-hour dark cycle.
todo_for_researcher: Specify the exact number of mice per group (n>=8 minimum)
steps.notes: Acclimation minimizes stress, which can impact physiological parameters like intestinal permeability. Age and strain are critical for experimental consistency.

steps.title: Randomize and Assign Mice to Groups
steps.body_md: Randomly assign the acclimatized C57BL/6 mice into two groups: one receiving LGG supplementation and one receiving the vehicle control. Ensure equal distribution of age and weight (if applicable) across groups. Each group should have a minimum of 8 mice.
steps.notes: Randomization is critical to prevent bias.

steps.title: Daily Oral Gavage of LGG Group
steps.body_md: Beginning on Day 1, orally administer 200 µL of the freshly prepared LGG suspension (at the determined CFU/day dose) to mice in the LGG group once daily for 4 consecutive weeks. Gently restrain each mouse and carefully introduce a sterile gavage needle into the esophagus, ensuring proper placement before dispensing the solution slowly to avoid aspiration.
todo_for_researcher: Confirm optimal gavage needle size for C57BL/6 mice
steps.notes: Consistent daily administration is crucial for the intervention's efficacy. Proper gavage technique is essential to minimize stress and prevent injury or aspiration.

steps.title: Daily Oral Gavage of Control Group
steps.body_md: Concurrently with the LGG group, orally administer 200 µL of sterile physiological saline to mice in the vehicle control group once daily for 4 consecutive weeks, using the same gavage technique.
todo_for_researcher: Confirm optimal gavage needle size for C57BL/6 mice
steps.notes: This group controls for the effects of the gavage procedure itself and the vehicle.

steps.title: Monitor Animal Health and Weight
steps.body_md: Throughout the 4-week supplementation period, monitor all mice daily for general health, body weight changes, and any signs of adverse effects (e.g., severe lethargy, changes in fur condition, diarrhea, or reduced food/water intake). Record observations.
steps.notes: Ensures animal welfare and allows for early detection of potential issues related to the intervention or gavage. Significant weight loss or illness may necessitate removal from the study.

steps.title: Prepare FITC-Dextran Solution
steps.body_md: Prepare a 60 mg/mL solution of FITC-dextran (4 kDa) in sterile PBS. Vortex thoroughly to ensure complete dissolution. Store in the dark at 4°C until use. This concentration is chosen to ensure sufficient detection in serum at the specified gavage volume.

steps.title: Fast Mice Prior to Gavage
steps.body_md: Fast all mice (both control and supplemented groups) for 4 hours prior to oral gavage with FITC-dextran. Ensure ad libitum access to water during the fasting period. Fasting ensures consistent gastric emptying and absorption kinetics.

steps.title: Administer FITC-Dextran by Oral Gavage
steps.body_md: Carefully restrain each mouse and administer 0.6 mL of the 60 mg/mL FITC-dextran solution (prepared in step 1) via oral gavage using a sterile gavage needle. Ensure accurate delivery and observe for any signs of aspiration. The gavage volume and concentration are selected to provide an adequate dose for detection in the serum.
todo_for_researcher: Specify appropriate gavage needle size for C57BL/6 mice.

steps.title: Post-Gavage Incubation
steps.body_md: After gavage, return mice to their cages with continued access to water (and food if appropriate per experimental design, though fasting for the full 4 hours before blood collection is recommended) for a 4-hour incubation period. This allows sufficient time for intestinal absorption and circulation of FITC-dextran.

steps.title: Perform Terminal Blood Collection
steps.body_md: At exactly 4 hours post-gavage, perform terminal blood collection from each mouse. Euthanize mice using an IACUC-approved method (e.g., CO2 asphyxiation followed by cervical dislocation). Collect blood via cardiac puncture or retro-orbital sinus into a sterile microcentrifuge tube, ensuring collection into plain tubes (no anticoagulant) if serum is desired, or tubes with anticoagulant (e.g., EDTA or heparin) if plasma is desired. Aim for maximum blood collection (typically 0.5-1 mL per mouse).
equipment_needed: Sterile microcentrifuge tubes (with or without anticoagulant)
Sterile needles and syringes (for blood collection)
todo_for_researcher: Confirm IACUC-approved euthanasia and blood collection protocols for C57BL/6 mice.

steps.title: Process Blood to Obtain Serum
steps.body_md: Allow collected blood to clot at room temperature for 30 minutes (if using plain tubes). Centrifuge blood samples at 2000 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C. Carefully aspirate the supernatant (serum) into new, labeled microcentrifuge tubes, avoiding disturbance of the red blood cell pellet. If working with plasma (collected using anticoagulant), centrifuge immediately after collection without clotting.

steps.title: Store Serum Samples
steps.body_md: Store isolated serum samples at -80°C until further analysis. Avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles, which can degrade the fluorophore or affect protein-FITC binding.

steps.title: Measure FITC-Dextran Fluorescence
steps.body_md: Thaw serum samples on ice. Pipette 100 µL of each serum sample into a black, opaque 96-well plate. Measure the fluorescence intensity using a fluorescence microplate reader with an excitation wavelength of 485 nm and an emission wavelength of 535 nm. Include a standard curve of known FITC-dextran concentrations in mouse serum (or PBS if serum matrix effects are negligible or accounted for) on each plate.
controls: FITC-dextran standard curve (in mouse serum or PBS)
todo_for_researcher: Determine appropriate range for FITC-dextran standard curve in mouse serum.
Confirm optimal gain settings for fluorescence plate reader.

steps.title: Calculate Intestinal Permeability
steps.body_md: Generate a standard curve from the known FITC-dextran concentrations and their measured fluorescence intensities. Use this standard curve to determine the concentration of FITC-dextran in each experimental serum sample. The serum concentration of FITC-dextran is directly proportional to intestinal permeability. Express results as µg/mL of FITC-dextran in serum or as fold change over vehicle control.
todo_for_researcher: Specify appropriate data analysis software (e.g., GraphPad Prism, Excel).

steps.title: Euthanize mice
steps.body_md: Euthanize mice according to institutional guidelines. The preferred method is CO2 inhalation in a gradually filled chamber. Ensure complete cessation of respiration and heartbeat before proceeding.
todo_for_researcher: Verify CO2 flow rate for gradual unconsciousness and euthanasia.

steps.title: Perform terminal blood collection
steps.body_md: Immediately after euthanasia, perform terminal blood collection. The preferred method is cardiac puncture. Collect blood using a syringe with a 23 gauge needle into a serum separator tube. Gently invert the tubes 5-6 times. Alternatively, perform retro-orbital blood collection if cardiac puncture is not feasible. This blood will be used for FITC-dextran analysis.
steps.notes: Ensure quick collection to prevent coagulation.

steps.title: Prepare blood for serum separation
steps.body_md: Allow the collected blood to clot at room temperature for 30 minutes. After clotting, centrifuge the serum separator tubes at 2000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C to separate serum.

steps.title: Store serum samples
steps.body_md: Carefully aliquot the clear serum supernatant into cryovials and immediately flash-freeze in liquid nitrogen. Store samples at -80°C until further analysis for FITC-dextran.
steps.notes: Avoid freeze-thaw cycles.

steps.title: Disinfect and open abdominal cavity
steps.body_md: Place the euthanized mouse in a supine position. Disinfect the lower abdomen thoroughly with a 70% ethanol solution. Make a vertical midline incision of approximately 5 cm through the skin and abdominal muscle layers to expose the abdominal cavity.
steps.notes: Ensure sterile technique where possible to prevent contamination of tissues if secondary analyses are planned.

steps.title: Locate and excise small intestine
steps.body_md: Gently move aside the organs to visualize the small intestine. Carefully disentangle the ileum from its mesenteric attachments and excise approximately 2 cm of the distal ileum using fine scissors. Place the ileum segment in a sterile dish on ice.
steps.notes: Minimize handling of the tissue to preserve its integrity.

steps.title: Flush and clean ileum segment
steps.body_md: Place the excised ileum segment on a sterile surface. Gently flush the lumen of the ileum with ice-cold phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) using a blunt-ended needle and syringe to remove intestinal contents. Ensure thorough cleaning without damaging the tissue.

steps.title: Collect and store ileum tissue
steps.body_md: Cut the cleaned ileum segment into two pieces. Flash-freeze one piece in liquid nitrogen for Western blot analysis (claudin-1, occludin). Place the other piece into a cryovial containing a suitable fixative (e.g., 10% neutral buffered formalin) if histology is required, otherwise flash-freeze for other analyses. Store flash-frozen samples at -80°C. If fixed, store at 4°C until further processing.
equipment_needed: Container with 10% neutral buffered formalin (if needed)
todo_for_researcher: Confirm whether histology is required for ileum and colon, and thus whether to fix tissue or only flash-freeze.

steps.title: Locate and excise colon
steps.body_md: After ileum collection, locate the colon. Carefully excise approximately 2 cm of the distal colon (excluding the cecum and rectum) using fine scissors. Place the colon segment in a sterile dish on ice.
steps.notes: Avoid contamination from fecal matter.

steps.title: Flush and clean colon segment
steps.body_md: Place the excised colon segment on a sterile surface. Gently flush the lumen of the colon with ice-cold phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) using a blunt-ended needle and syringe to remove intestinal contents. Ensure thorough cleaning without damaging the tissue.

steps.title: Collect and store colon tissue
steps.body_md: Cut the cleaned colon segment into two pieces. Flash-freeze one piece in liquid nitrogen for Western blot analysis (claudin-1, occludin). Place the other piece into a cryovial containing a suitable fixative (e.g., 10% neutral buffered formalin) if histology is required, otherwise flash-freeze for other analyses. Store flash-frozen samples at -80°C. If fixed, store at 4°C until further processing.
equipment_needed: Container with 10% neutral buffered formalin (if needed)
todo_for_researcher: Confirm whether histology is required for ileum and colon, and thus whether to fix tissue or only flash-freeze.

steps.title: Prepare FITC-Dextran Standards
steps.body_md: Prepare a series of FITC-dextran (4 kDa) standards ranging from 0.05 µg/mL to 50 µg/mL using pooled mouse serum (from untreated mice or a commercial source) as the diluent. Make at least 7-8 serial dilutions. Ensure accuracy in pipetting. Prepare each standard in duplicate.
todo_for_researcher: Determine the exact number and concentration of standards to create a robust standard curve based on expected sample concentrations.
steps.notes: Using pooled mouse serum as the diluent helps account for potential fluorescence interference from serum components, providing a more accurate standard curve for serum samples.

steps.title: Plate Standards and Samples
steps.body_md: Pipette 100 µL of each prepared FITC-dextran standard into designated wells of a black, opaque 96-well plate. Ensure standards are plated in duplicate or triplicate. Subsequently, pipette 100 µL of each unknown serum sample (collected from C57BL/6 mice) into separate wells. Ensure samples are also plated in duplicate. Include wells with 100 µL of pooled mouse serum only (without FITC-dextran) as a blank.
steps.notes: Black, opaque plates minimize well-to-well crosstalk and background fluorescence, which is crucial for sensitive fluorescence measurements.

steps.title: Measure Fluorescence
steps.body_md: Place the 96-well plate into a fluorescence plate reader. Set the excitation wavelength to 485 nm and the emission wavelength to 528 nm. Measure the fluorescence intensity for all wells. Ensure the gain setting of the plate reader is optimized to maximize signal differentiation without saturating the detector. The plate should remain at room temperature during measurement.
todo_for_researcher: Specify the exact model of fluorescence plate reader and any specific software settings required.
steps.notes: The specified excitation/emission wavelengths are standard for FITC-dextran. Optimizing gain is critical for accurate quantification across the wide range of concentrations.

steps.title: Generate Standard Curve
steps.body_md: Subtract the average fluorescence of the blank wells (pooled mouse serum only) from all standard and sample readings. Plot the average corrected fluorescence intensity of the FITC-dextran standards against their known concentrations to generate a standard curve. Typically, a linear or sigmoidal fit (e.g., a 4-parameter logistic curve) is appropriate, ensuring a high R-squared value (>0.99).
equipment_needed: Computer with graphing software (e.g., GraphPad Prism, Excel)
todo_for_researcher: Specify the preferred graphing software and data analysis method (e.g., linear regression, 4PL).
steps.notes: A robust standard curve is essential for accurately interpolating the concentrations of unknown samples. Correcting for background fluorescence from serum blanks improves accuracy.

steps.title: Calculate Sample Concentrations
steps.body_md: Use the generated standard curve equation to interpolate the FITC-dextran concentration (µg/mL) for each unknown serum sample based on its corrected fluorescence intensity reading. Report concentrations for all replicates and then average them for each sample.
equipment_needed: Computer with graphing software (e.g., GraphPad Prism, Excel)
steps.notes: Record raw fluorescence values and calculated concentrations for all samples for future troubleshooting or re-analysis.

steps.title: Tissue Homogenization
steps.body_md: Upon receipt of intestinal tissue (ileum and/or colon segments), immediately place tissue on ice. Add 500 μL of RIPA lysis buffer (containing protease and phosphatase inhibitors) to the tissue. Homogenize the tissue using a tissue homogenizer (e.g., Bead Ruptor 24 Elite) at 4.0 m/s for 30 seconds, repeating for 3 cycles with 1-minute intervals on ice between cycles to prevent overheating.
todo_for_researcher: Identify specific cat# and vendor for protease and phosphatase inhibitor cocktails suitable for intestinal tissue proteomics.
steps.notes: Ensure all buffers are pre-chilled and kept on ice during the entire process to prevent protein degradation.

steps.title: Cell Lysis and Centrifugation
steps.body_md: Following homogenization, incubate the samples on ice for 30 minutes to facilitate complete cell lysis. Afterward, centrifuge the homogenates at 12,000 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C to pellet cellular debris.
steps.notes: The supernatant contains the total protein extract.

steps.title: Protein Quantification
steps.body_md: Carefully transfer the supernatant (containing the protein lysate) to a new pre-chilled microcentrifuge tube. Determine the protein concentration using the BCA protein assay according to the manufacturer's instructions. Prepare a standard curve using bovine serum albumin (BSA).
steps.notes: Accurate protein quantification is critical for equal loading in subsequent SDS-PAGE.

assumptions: All animal work will be conducted under an approved IACUC protocol.
Standard vivarium facilities and experienced animal handlers are available.
All necessary reagents and equipment for molecular biology (Western blot) and fluorescence assays are available.
Researchers are proficient in oral gavage and tissue dissection techniques.

gaps: Cell density of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG (LGG) culture was not specified, making calculation of total quantity impossible.
The total number of mice for the experiment (and thus the total amount of food/water/bedding/reagents needed) was not provided.

The number of weeks for oral supplementation was specified as 4 weeks, but the frequency of gavage was daily, but total volume for LGG and vehicle control is given as 200uL *per dose*. The frequency and duration of use makes calculation of total quantity feasible but needs number of mice. Without knowing the number of mice, the total amount of LGG, physiological saline, and FITC-dextran cannot be precisely calculated. The calculation for the FITC-Dextran solution requires detailed protocol of animal numbers to calculate the total amount required (0.6 mL per mouse, for a given number of mice).
